_comment: Single source of truth for constants that exist on both the JS and PHP sides. Imported via JSON in src/components/constants.js (webpack handles JSON natively) and read via motion_blocks_shared_constants() in animation-plugin.php. Adding a new shared constant: add it here, then expose it through both consumer paths.
_README_attributeDefaults: === Animation-attribute defaults: the dual-layer model ===

There are TWO layers of 'default' for animation attributes. Both are needed; both serve a distinct job. The split is intentional. Before changing any default value, know which layer you're touching and what ripples.
--- Layer 1: ACTIVE DEFAULTS (this file, key 'attributeDefaults') ---
Read by:
- JS — DEFAULT_ATTRIBUTES in src/components/constants.js spreads this object, then layers JS-only keys on top.
- PHP — motion_blocks_attr_default('key') helper in animation-plugin.php; called everywhere the render filter does $attrs[key] ?? motion_blocks_attr_default('key').
Determines:
- What selectMode() writes for new blocks.
- What the PHP render filter substitutes when a key is missing from the saved block-comment JSON.

Safe to change: yes. Edits here flow to both JS and PHP automatically. Change-blast radius is bounded by Layer 2's freeze (see below) — existing blocks with keys omitted from their saved JSON keep resolving to whatever Layer 2 says, not what's here.
--- Layer 2: SCHEMA DEFAULTS (src/index.js → addAnimationAttributes) ---
Read by:
- WordPress's block-comment serializer — uses each attribute's schema default to decide what to OMIT from the saved JSON (values equal to the schema default get dropped to save bytes).
- WordPress's block-comment deserializer — substitutes the schema default for any key missing from saved JSON when reading a block back into the editor.
Determines:
- Which attribute values get omitted from the saved post_content.
- What an editor read resolves to for legacy blocks whose JSON pre-dates a default change.

Safe to change: rarely. Changing a schema default re-resolves every legacy block where the user accepted that default at save time — their effective value silently changes on plugin update. (Validation no longer breaks because post-cad8cf2 save() emits pure core HTML with no animation markup, but the perceived behavior shift is real.) Some schema defaults are INTENTIONALLY frozen at historical values to insulate legacy blocks — see the inline comment on animationDelay in addAnimationAttributes for the canonical example (schema=0.4 vs active=0).
--- Rule of thumb ---
To change new-block defaults + render-time fallbacks: edit this file. Both JS and PHP follow.
To change what gets omitted from saved JSON or how legacy blocks resolve: edit the schema in src/index.js, accepting the ripple to legacy blocks.
Active and schema MAY diverge for an attribute; some do (animationDelay). When they do, an inline comment at the schema callsite explains why.
--- Out of scope: legacy migration fallbacks ---

motion_blocks_migrate_scroll_appear_attrs in animation-plugin.php uses LITERAL fallback values (e.g. animationDelay ?? 0.4). Those are pre-slot-model defaults captured for back-compat with legacy data — do NOT switch them to motion_blocks_attr_default(). The function's job is to reproduce the perceived behavior of blocks saved before the slot-model refactor, regardless of how current defaults evolve. See the comment at the top of that function.
